rootCause.summary: The server returned an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error when the user accessed the `/status_codes/500` endpoint.
triggerChain: User clicked 'Status Codes' on the homepage.
User navigated to the Status Codes page.
User clicked the '500' link on the Status Codes page (inferred).
Server responded with HTTP 500 Internal Server Error.
evidence: The final user interaction (click event at timestamp 1775990958709) occurred on 'pageUrl': 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/status_codes/500', indicating the user had successfully navigated to a page explicitly designed to return a 500 status code.
rootCause.fix: Investigate the server-side application code or infrastructure responsible for handling requests to `/status_codes/500` to identify and resolve the root cause of the 500 error. Ensure proper error handling and logging are in place. (Note: For `the-internet.herokuapp.com`, this behavior is intentional for testing purposes.)

bugReport.title: Server returns HTTP 500 Internal Server Error for /status_codes/500 endpoint
bugReport.description: The user encountered an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error when navigating to the `/status_codes/500` page. This indicates a server-side issue prevented the page from loading correctly. While this specific endpoint on `the-internet.herokuapp.com` is designed to demonstrate a 500 error, in a production application, this would represent a critical failure.
timeline: ~1775990955000 (inferred): User navigated to 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/status_codes/500' (likely by clicking a '500' link on the Status Codes page), and the server responded with HTTP 500.
1775990958709: User clicked the 'here' link on the 'https://the-internet.herokuapp.com/status_codes/500' page.
bugReport.networkActivity: One XHR request to 'https://298279967.log.optimizely.com/event' failed with 'status: 0' and 'error: Network error'. This is likely a tracking call and not directly related to the 500 page error. The critical network event (GET /status_codes/500 returning 500) is not explicitly recorded as an XHR but is inferred from the page URL.
bugReport.consoleLogs: No relevant console errors or warnings were recorded.

environment.userAgent: Mozilla/5.0 (Windows NT 10.0; Win64; x64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/146.0.0.0 Safari/537.36

steps: Click on the 'Status Codes' link from the list.
On the 'Status Codes' page, click on the '500' link.
reproductionSteps.expectedBehavior: In a production application, the page should load successfully with a 200 OK status, or a user-friendly error page should be displayed if a server error occurs, without returning a raw 500 status code directly to the browser.
reproductionSteps.actualBehavior: The server returns an HTTP 500 Internal Server Error, displaying a generic server error page.
